original_hypothesis.title: Tax Rate Above 100% Causes Unchecked Underflow in _taxProcessing
original_hypothesis.hypothesis_description: setProjectTaxRates has no upper bound validation. If projectBuyTaxBasisPoints or projectSellTaxBasisPoints is set >= BP_DENOM (10000), the computed tax in _taxProcessing exceeds sentAmount_. The entire _taxProcessing block is unchecked, so `amountLessTax_ -= tax` underflows to near uint256.max, minting astronomical tokens to the recipient.
original_hypothesis.investigation_rationale: The unchecked block at _taxProcessing means if tax > sentAmount_, the subtraction wraps. The recipient receives ~2^256 tokens. This is a catastrophic inflation bug triggered by a missing input validation in setProjectTaxRates.

proof_of_signal.pattern: Entire _taxProcessing in unchecked block; amountLessTax_ -= tax underflows if tax > sentAmount_; no BP_DENOM cap on tax rates in setProjectTaxRates

preconditions: Owner or factory sets tax basis points >= 10000
A buy or sell transaction occurs through LP

initial_code_evidence.vulnerable_pattern: unchecked { amountLessTax_ -= tax; } where tax can exceed sentAmount_
initial_code_evidence.specific_concern: setProjectTaxRates accepts any uint16 value (up to 65535 BP = 655%) without validation

analysis_questions: Can the factory (not just owner) set tax rates? What constraints does factory have?
Would the underflowed amountLessTax_ cause _balances[to] += to exceed totalSupply?
What is the blast radius of infinite token inflation?
validation_requirements: Set tax to 10001 BP, execute a sell, verify amountLessTax_ underflows
Verify no SafeMath wrapper on the unchecked block

original_hypothesis.preliminary_attack_scenario: Owner/factory calls setProjectTaxRates(10001, 10001). On next sell to LP: tax = amount * 10001 / 10000 > amount. amountLessTax_ underflows to ~2^256. Recipient balance inflates to near-max uint256.
prerequisites: Owner or factory sets tax rate >= 10000 BP

potential_impact_if_confirmed.financial_impact: Complete destruction of token economy; infinite token minting

original_hypothesis.test_coverage_gap: No boundary test for tax rates near or above BP_DENOM

technical_investigation_focus.execution_timing: Two transactions: setProjectTaxRates + any LP trade

validation_result.impact_summary: When projectBuyTaxBasisPoints or projectSellTaxBasisPoints is set >= 10000 (BP_DENOM), the computed tax exceeds sentAmount_ in the unchecked _taxProcessing block, causing amountLessTax_ to underflow to near uint256.max. On buys, this inflates the recipient's balance to near-max uint256. On sells to an LP with existing balance, the checked addition overflows and reverts (DoS).
validation_result.detailed_analysis: The defect consists of two code-level issues that combine: (1) `setProjectTaxRates` accepts any uint16 value (0-65535) without validating that the rate is below BP_DENOM (10000), and (2) the entire `_taxProcessing` function body is wrapped in an `unchecked` block. When tax basis points >= 10000, the formula `(sentAmount_ * taxBasisPoints) / BP_DENOM` produces a tax value >= sentAmount_. The subsequent `amountLessTax_ -= tax` underflows in the unchecked block, wrapping to a value near uint256.max. Back in `_transfer`, the line `_balances[to] += amountMinusTax` (which is NOT in an unchecked block) either: (a) succeeds if _balances[to] is 0 (on a buy, setting the buyer's balance to ~uint256.max), or (b) reverts with overflow if _balances[to] > 0 (typical for LP on a sell). The `_balances[address(this)] += tax` inside the unchecked block also silently inflates the contract's tracked balance beyond what totalSupply reflects, breaking supply conservation. The comment on `setProjectTaxRates` says 'subject to only ever decreasing' but the code enforces no such constraint, nor any upper bound.
validation_result.code_evidence_assessment: Direct code evidence confirms both the missing validation and the underflow path. setProjectTaxRates at AgentToken.sol (around line 261-274) has no require or if statement checking the new values. _taxProcessing at AgentToken.sol (around line 425-460) wraps its entire body in `unchecked {}`. The subtraction `amountLessTax_ -= tax` at the end of the unchecked block is the exact underflow point. The _transfer function at AgentToken.sol uses the returned amountMinusTax in checked arithmetic (`_balances[to] += amountMinusTax`), meaning the underflowed value either inflates balances (when to has 0 balance) or causes revert (when to has nonzero balance).

vulnerable_code_snippets.vulnerability_explanation: No validation that newProjectBuyTaxBasisPoints_ < BP_DENOM (10000) or newProjectSellTaxBasisPoints_ < BP_DENOM. Accepts any uint16 value (up to 65535 = 655.35%).
Entire body is in unchecked block. When taxBasisPoints >= BP_DENOM, tax > sentAmount_, so `amountLessTax_ -= tax` underflows to near uint256.max.

The underflowed amountMinusTax (near uint256.max) is added to _balances[to] in checked arithmetic. If to has 0 balance, this succeeds and inflates their balance astronomically. If to has nonzero balance, the addition overflows and reverts (DoS).

calling_sequence: 1. Owner/factory calls setProjectTaxRates(10001, 10001) - no validation, rates set to 10001 BP
2. User initiates a buy via Uniswap (router calls transferFrom on AgentToken)
6. amountLessTax_ = amount - tax underflows to ~uint256.max in unchecked block
8. If buyer had 0 balance, their balance is now ~uint256.max - catastrophic inflation
execution_path_analysis.vulnerable_code_path: The path from setProjectTaxRates to the underflow is: setProjectTaxRates sets projectBuyTaxBasisPoints/projectSellTaxBasisPoints to >= 10000 (no validation at AgentToken.sol:269-270). On subsequent transfer involving LP (AgentToken.sol:343 transfer or :364 transferFrom), _transfer is called with applyTax=true (AgentToken.sol:344,366). Inside _transfer (AgentToken.sol:389), _taxProcessing is called. At AgentToken.sol:431, the unchecked block begins. At ~line 438, projectTax = (sentAmount_ * projectSellTaxBasisPoints) / BP_DENOM exceeds sentAmount_ when projectSellTaxBasisPoints >= BP_DENOM. At ~line 451, amountLessTax_ -= tax underflows. This underflowed value is returned and used at AgentToken.sol:392 as `_balances[to] += amountMinusTax`.

config_validation.deployment_evidence: Deployment scripts (v2/deployFactory.ts, v2/deployVIPFactory.ts, v2/deployMigrator.ts) set tax params via process.env.TAX and process.env.SWAP_THRESHOLD. While these likely use reasonable values, there is no on-chain enforcement preventing future changes via setProjectTaxRates or setTokenTaxParams to values >= 10000. The defect is in the missing validation in the smart contract code itself.

validation_result.attack_scenario_validation: The attack scenario is validated: (1) Owner or factory calls setProjectTaxRates(10001, 10001) - this succeeds as there is no validation check. (2) A buy transaction occurs from LP to buyer. (3) In _taxProcessing (unchecked block), tax = sentAmount * 10001 / 10000 > sentAmount. (4) amountLessTax_ -= tax wraps to ~uint256.max. (5) _balances[buyer] += amountMinusTax sets buyer balance to ~uint256.max (if buyer started with 0). For sells, _balances[LP] += amountMinusTax would overflow in checked arithmetic and revert, causing DoS. The defect is confirmed regardless of whether the privileged caller is malicious (rug pull) or makes a configuration error - the code should not allow parameters that break its own invariants.
validation_result.impact_assessment: CRITICAL: This is a missing input validation that enables catastrophic outcomes. If tax rates are set >= 100%, buys result in near-infinite token balance for the buyer (complete destruction of token economics), and sells are DoS'd (checked overflow revert). Even with the onlyOwnerOrFactory restriction, this is a code defect - the function's own NatSpec states rates should 'only ever decrease' but enforces nothing. Additionally, the factory admin can set _tokenTaxParams with values >= 10000 via setTokenTaxParams (no validation there either), affecting all newly created tokens.
recommended_mitigations: Add validation in setProjectTaxRates: require(newProjectBuyTaxBasisPoints_ < BP_DENOM && newProjectSellTaxBasisPoints_ < BP_DENOM, 'Tax rate too high');
Add validation in _processTaxParams during initialize to reject taxBasisPoints >= BP_DENOM
Enforce monotonic decrease as documented: require(newProjectBuyTaxBasisPoints_ <= projectBuyTaxBasisPoints && newProjectSellTaxBasisPoints_ <= projectSellTaxBasisPoints, 'Tax can only decrease');
Add validation in factory setTokenTaxParams to prevent encoding values >= 10000 for tax basis points
Consider removing the unchecked block from _taxProcessing, or at minimum add an explicit check that tax <= sentAmount_ before the subtraction
validation_result.replication_plan: 1. Deploy AgentToken via factory with standard tax params (e.g., 200 BP buy/sell). 2. Add initial liquidity. 3. Owner calls setProjectTaxRates(10001, 10001). 4. From a fresh address (0 balance), perform a buy via Uniswap pair. 5. Verify buyer's balance is near uint256.max. 6. Attempt a sell and verify it reverts due to checked overflow on LP balance update.
uncertainties: The exact behavior depends on whether _balances[to] is zero or nonzero at the time of the underflowed addition - zero results in inflation, nonzero results in revert
In practice, the owner/factory are privileged roles; the severity depends on the trust model for these roles

The factory's setTokenTaxParams uses uint256 parameters that are truncated to uint16 in _processTaxParams - values > 65535 would be silently truncated, but values 10000-65535 pass through correctly and trigger the bug
additional_testing_needed: Fuzz test setProjectTaxRates with values near and above BP_DENOM boundary
End-to-end test: set tax to 10001, perform buy, verify balance inflation
End-to-end test: set tax to 10001, perform sell, verify revert
Test tax = 10000 exactly (100%): amountLessTax_ would be 0, which is a valid but pathological edge case
Verify that factory-deployed tokens with _tokenTaxParams containing BP >= 10000 exhibit the same issue at initialization
